key_insight: WarpagePINN uses a two-stage physics-informed approach to predict chiplet temperature and thermal warpage without labeled simulation data.
engineering_relevance: It offers a fast, physics-grounded tool for advanced packaging design, where CTE mismatch-driven warpage can threaten reliability and assembly yield.
method: Stage 1: Fourier-series temperature model trained only with PDE-based loss while inherently satisfying boundary conditions.
Stage 2: MLP warpage predictor trained with a hybrid supervisory strategy that optimizes an energy-based loss rather than residual loss.
Parametric extension quantifies uncertainty/sensitivity to CTE variations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports close agreement with finite element results, with MAE of 0.2 μm and about 1000× speedup in CTE parameterization studies.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of the physics assumptions and problem setup, so validation is important when extending to new package geometries, materials, or boundary conditions.
paper_title: WarpagePINN: Thermal Warpage Prediction in Advanced Packaging via a Two-Stage Physics-Informed Neural Networks
